items.question: Which of the following represents the formula for Scaled Dot-Product Attention presented in the text?
items.explanation: The excerpt explicitly states the formula is 'Attention(Q, K, V) = softmax( QK^T / √d_k )V'.

items.question: In the Scaled Dot-Product Attention formula, what does the variable 'd_k' represent?
options: The depth of the input tokens
The total number of attention heads
The dimension of the keys (and queries)
The scaling factor for the values
items.explanation: The notation '√d_k' is used in the formula as the scaling factor. In the context of attention mechanisms described in this paper, 'd_k' refers to the dimensionality of the keys.

items.question: What mathematical operation is applied to the matrix product QK^T before it is multiplied by V?
options: It is scaled by √d_k and passed through a softmax
It is multiplied by the Value matrix immediately
It is normalized by a ReLU activation
It is divided by the square root of the model size
items.explanation: The formula shows the term 'softmax( (QK^T) / √d_k )'. This indicates the dot product is scaled by the inverse square root of d_k and then the softmax function is applied.
